content:
# Philippians Chapter 2 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph), PDF pages 195-196 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/PHP_002_page_001.jpg, PHP_002_page_002.jpg

---

**1.** If therefore [there is] any encouragement in Christ, if any consolation of love, if any fellowship of [the] Spirit, if any compassions and mercies,

**2.** fill-full my joy, that the same thing you-might-think, having the same love, joined-in-soul, the one thing thinking,

**3.** nothing according-to selfish-ambition nor according-to vainglory, but in the lowliness-of-mind one-another considering-as-surpassing yourselves,

**4.** not the [things] of yourselves each looking-at, but also the [things] of others each [looking-at].

**5.** For this think in you which also in Christ Jesus,

**6.** who in [the] form of God existing, not robbery considered the being equal with God,

**7.** but himself he-emptied, [the] form of a slave having-taken, in [the] likeness of men having-become; and in outward-appearance having-been-found as a man,

**8.** he-humbled himself, having-become obedient unto death, and death of a cross.

**9.** Therefore also God him highly-exalted and granted to him the name the [one] above every name,

**10.** that in the name of Jesus every knee should-bow, of [those in] heaven and of [those] on earth and of [those] under-the-earth,

**11.** and every tongue should-confess that Lord [is] Jesus Christ, to [the] glory of God [the] Father.

**12.** So-then, my beloved-ones, just-as you-always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, with fear and trembling your-own salvation work-out;

**13.** for God is the [one] working in you both the willing and the working for the [sake of his] good-pleasure.

**14.** All-things do without grumblings and disputes,

**15.** that you-might-become blameless and unmixed, children of God unblemished in [the] midst of a crooked and having-been-perverted generation, among whom you-shine as luminaries in [the] world,

**16.** [the] word of life holding-forth, for a boast to me for [the] day of Christ, that not in vain I-ran nor in vain I-labored.

**17.** But if even I-am-being-poured-out-as-a-drink-offering upon the sacrifice and priestly-service of your faith, I-rejoice and I-rejoice-together with all of you;

**18.** and the same also you rejoice and rejoice-together with me.

**19.** But I-hope in [the] Lord Jesus Timothy to-send quickly to you, that I-also might-be-of-good-spirit having-known the [things] concerning you.

**20.** For no-one I-have like-souled, who genuinely the [things] concerning you will-care-for;

**21.** for the all the [things] of themselves they-seek, not the [things] of Christ Jesus.

**22.** But the proven-character of him you-know, that as a father [with] a child, with me he-served-as-a-slave for the gospel.

**23.** This-one therefore I-hope to-send, as soon-as I-might-see-clearly the [things] concerning me, immediately;

**24.** but I-have-confidence in [the] Lord that I myself also quickly will-come.

**25.** But necessary I-considered Epaphroditus, the brother and fellow-worker and fellow-soldier of me, but your apostle and minister of my need, to-send to you,

**26.** since-indeed he-was longing-for you all, and being-distressed because you-heard that he-was-sick.

**27.** For indeed he-was-sick, close to death; but God had-mercy-on him, and not him only, but also me, that not sorrow upon sorrow I-might-have.

**28.** More-eagerly therefore I-sent him, that having-seen him again you-might-rejoice, and I might-be less-sorrowful.

**29.** Receive therefore him in [the] Lord with all joy, and the such-ones hold-in-honor,

**30.** because on-account-of the work of Christ unto death he-drew-near, having-risked the life, that he-might-fill-up your lack of the toward me priestly-service.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript Observations (Codex Sinaiticus)

1. **v. 1 -- ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΟΣ (Spirit):** Sinaiticus reads ΚΟΙΝΩΝΙΑ ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΟΣ ("fellowship of Spirit"). No article before ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΟΣ in the manuscript; the article is supplied in brackets in translation for English clarity.

2. **v. 4 -- ΕΚΑΣΤΟΙ (each):** Sinaiticus reads the plural ΕΚΑΣΤΟΙ rather than the singular ΕΚΑΣΤΟΣ found in many later manuscripts. This plural form is the harder reading and likely original.

3. **v. 5 -- ΤΟΥΤΟ ΦΡΟΝΕΙΤΕ:** The manuscript reads ΤΟΥΤΟ ΦΡΟΝΕΙΤΕ ΕΝ ΥΜΙΝ Ο ΚΑΙ ΕΝ ΧΡΙΣΤΩ ΙΗΣΟΥ. The relative pronoun Ο lacks an explicit verb; "[was]" or "[is]" must be supplied. I have left the clause without a supplied verb to preserve the ambiguity of the Greek construction, which may be either indicative ("which was also in Christ Jesus") or imperative ("which [let be] also in Christ Jesus").

4. **v. 6 -- ΑΡΠΑΓΜΟΝ (robbery/seizure):** Sinaiticus reads ΑΡΠΑΓΜΟΝ. This word is notoriously difficult. It can mean (a) a thing seized, (b) a thing to be seized, or (c) an act of robbery. I translate "robbery" to preserve the abstract noun form. The theological weight of this term is enormous: whether Christ regarded equality with God as something already possessed (and not clung to) or something not yet possessed (and not grasped at) depends on interpreting this single word.

5. **v. 7 -- ΚΕΝΟΩ (he-emptied):** Sinaiticus reads ΕΑΥΤΟΝ ΕΚΕΝΩΣΕΝ. The aorist active is preserved. "He-emptied himself" -- the famous kenosis. The text does not specify of what he emptied himself; the following participles (having-taken, having-become) are modal/circumstantial, explaining the manner of the emptying.

6. **v. 8 -- ΘΑΝΑΤΟΥ ΔΕ ΣΤΑΥΡΟΥ (and death of a cross):** Sinaiticus includes this phrase. The ΔΕ is mildly adversative or continuative: "and [indeed] death of a cross." The starkness of ΣΤΑΥΡΟΥ (cross) as a genitive qualifier is preserved.

7. **v. 9 -- ΥΠΕΡΥΨΩΣΕΝ (highly-exalted):** Compound verb with ΥΠΕΡ prefix, indicating exaltation beyond or above. Sinaiticus reads this clearly. The aorist tense is preserved.

8. **v. 11 -- ΚΥΡΙΟΣ ΙΗΣΟΥΣ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΣ:** Sinaiticus reads the full three-name confession. The nominative ΚΥΡΙΟΣ functions as predicate: "Lord [is] Jesus Christ." The copula is not present in the Greek.

9. **v. 15 -- ΑΚΕΡΑΙΟΙ (unmixed):** Literally "unmixed" or "unadulterated" (from alpha-privative + ΚΕΡΑΝΝΥΜΙ, "to mix"). Often smoothed to "innocent" or "harmless" in English translations, but the literal sense is of something pure and uncontaminated.

10. **v. 17 -- ΣΠΕΝΔΟΜΑΙ (I-am-being-poured-out-as-a-drink-offering):** Present passive indicative. The drink-offering metaphor (σπονδη) refers to the libation poured over a sacrifice. Paul's life is the libation; the Philippians' faith is the sacrifice. The present tense indicates an ongoing process.

11. **v. 21 -- ΟΙ ΠΑΝΤΕΣ (the all):** Sinaiticus reads ΟΙ ΠΑΝΤΕΣ with the article, emphasizing "they all" -- a sweeping indictment of Paul's companions except Timothy.

12. **v. 25 -- ΑΠΟΣΤΟΛΟΝ (apostle):** Used here in its non-technical sense of "sent-one" or "messenger." Epaphroditus is the Philippians' envoy, not an apostle in the Twelve sense. I retain the transliterated "apostle" to preserve the Greek word.

13. **v. 30 -- ΠΑΡΑΒΟΛΕΥΣΑΜΕΝΟΣ (having-risked):** Sinaiticus reads ΠΑΡΑΒΟΛΕΥΣΑΜΕΝΟΣ rather than the variant ΠΑΡΑΒΟΥΛΕΥΣΑΜΕΝΟΣ found in some manuscripts. The word means "having gambled" or "having risked" -- Epaphroditus wagered his life. This is the reading of the earliest witnesses.

14. **v. 30 -- ΤΟ ΕΡΓΟΝ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ (the work of Christ):** Sinaiticus reads ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ here. Some manuscripts read ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ("of the Lord"). The Sinaiticus reading is followed.

### Variant Summary

| Verse | Sinaiticus Reading | Notable Variants |
|-------|-------------------|------------------|
| 2:1 | ΕΙ ΤΙΣ ΚΟΙΝΩΝΙΑ ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΟΣ | Some MSS add ΑΓΙΟΥ |
| 2:4 | ΕΚΑΣΤΟΙ | ΕΚΑΣΤΟΣ (later MSS) |
| 2:5 | Ο ΚΑΙ | Some MSS read Α ΚΑΙ |
| 2:7 | ΕΝ ΟΜΟΙΩΜΑΤΙ ΑΝΘΡΩΠΩΝ ΓΕΝΟΜΕΝΟΣ | Word order varies in some witnesses |
| 2:9 | ΤΟ ΟΝΟΜΑ ΤΟ ΥΠΕΡ ΠΑΝ ΟΝΟΜΑ | ΤΟ with article before ΥΠΕΡ in Sinaiticus |
| 2:21 | ΟΙ ΠΑΝΤΕΣ ΓΑΡ ΤΑ ΕΑΥΤΩΝ ΖΗΤΟΥΣΙΝ | Majority text similar |
| 2:25 | ΑΠΟΣΤΟΛΟΝ | No significant variants |
| 2:30 | ΠΑΡΑΒΟΛΕΥΣΑΜΕΝΟΣ ΤΗ ΨΥΧΗ | ΠΑΡΑΒΟΥΛΕΥΣΑΜΕΝΟΣ in some MSS |
| 2:30 | ΤΟ ΕΡΓΟΝ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ | ΚΥΡΙΟΥ in some MSS |

---

*Translated from the manuscript as read. Fidelity to the source text governs all decisions.*

-- EMET
